pi_items.source: Code analysis: task.json description (line 5), setup_task.sh (line 45), export_result.sh (line 25), verifier.py (line 47) all reference this exact channel name

Code analysis: task.json description states 'listen on TCP port 6661 (MLLP)', setup_task.sh line 46 confirms 'TCP Listener @ 6661', setup_nextgen_connect.sh line 84 shows Docker port mapping '-p 6661:6661'
Code analysis: task.json description states 'send messages to a closed TCP port (localhost:9999)', setup_task.sh line 47 confirms 'TCP Sender @ localhost:9999 (Must FAIL)'

Code analysis: task.json description specifies archive directory as '/tmp/failed_messages/', setup_task.sh line 24 creates directory 'docker exec nextgen-connect mkdir -p /tmp/failed_messages', export_result.sh line 71 retrieves archived files from this directory

Code analysis: task.json description specifies 'Queue Messages: Never', setup_task.sh line 48 shows 'Queue: Never', export_result.sh line 39-40 checks for '<queueConnector>false</queueConnector>' which corresponds to NO_QUEUE setting in NextGen Connect UI
Code analysis: task.json description references '/home/ga/sample.hl7', setup_task.sh lines 13-18 explicitly create this file with HL7 v2.5 ADT^A01 message containing MSH, EVN, PID, and PV1 segments

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims verified as accurate. The task configures a NextGen Connect healthcare integration channel named 'Fail_Safe_Archiver' that listens on TCP port 6661 (MLLP protocol) and attempts to send messages to a deliberately inaccessible destination (localhost:9999). The channel uses a 'Fail Fast' configuration with queue messaging disabled and retry count set to 0. A JavaScript Post-Processor script archives failed messages to /tmp/failed_messages/ directory. The sample HL7 message used is synthetic test data (HL7 v2.5 format) created within the task setup, not from a real patient database.
